suggestions.signal_name: NFCI (Chicago Fed National Financial Conditions Index)
suggestions.observation: During the HY-IG Wave 2 exploratory pass, NFCI correlated 0.74 with HY-IG spread changes at monthly frequency over 2000-2023 and Granger-caused SPY 21d forward returns at p=0.02, lag 2.
suggestions.rationale: NFCI aggregates roughly 100 financial-conditions indicators into a single composite; if HY-IG spread proxies one slice of that composite, NFCI might serve as a broader-conditions variant for a separate NFCI -> SPY pair or as a regime overlay for HY-IG v3.
suggestions.possible_use_case: new pair (NFCI -> SPY); regime overlay for HY-IG v3
suggestions.caveats: NFCI is itself constructed from overlapping financial indicators (HY-IG spread may be an input), so direct inclusion on the HY-IG page would be double-counting. Sample pre-2000 is thin.
suggestions.notes: Observed during Wave 2 exploratory; flagged here rather than shipped to avoid ECON-SD scope violation.

suggestions.observation: Narrative review noted that 10Y-3M inversion episodes (1989, 2000, 2006, 2019) each preceded HY-IG spread widening by 6-12 months with roughly 80% hit rate across the 4 episodes.
suggestions.rationale: The term structure carries recession-probability information that complements credit-market stress. A dedicated Yield Curve -> SPY pair (or a Yield Curve -> HY-IG chain pair) could pick up signal 6+ months earlier than HY-IG alone.
suggestions.possible_use_case: new pair (yield_curve_10y_3m -> SPY); long-lead confirmer in cross-pair comparison
suggestions.caveats: 4 episodes is a very small sample; 2022 inversion did not (yet) generate a comparable HY-IG widening, so the relationship may be breaking down. Needs proper Cox-regression survival analysis before taking strategy weight.
suggestions.notes: Mentioned in the Wave 2 Story narrative as a cross-reference; captured here so it is not lost.

notes: Reference-instance example for ECON-AS (Wave 7A). Realistic HY-IG v2 suggestions; not the live registry. Illustrates (a) cross-agent filing (evan and ray both proposed), (b) honest caveats, (c) no status / lifecycle fields.
